title: TRACKER: migrate bridge from embedded Dolt to server mode so tuskd hooks pass
description:
Context:
- The bridge repo tracker is currently in embedded-Dolt mode.
- tuskd and the repo hooks assume server mode, so hook-backed commit flows reject otherwise-valid work.
- This is the bridge-local downstream repair lane corresponding to tusk-154.

Goal:
Migrate the bridge tracker from embedded Dolt to server mode without losing local issue history, then verify the normal hook path works again.

Scope:
- back up the current .beads state before migration
- reinitialize the tracker in server mode
- preserve or restore bridge issue data and local history as needed
- verify bd reports server mode and a trivial commit path no longer fails on the tracker substrate

Non-goals:
- changing tuskd hook policy
- moving bridge workflow ownership back into tusk
- unrelated spec or flake edits except what the migration strictly requires

Verification:
- bd dolt status reports server mode
- a no-op or trivial commit path passes the bridge hooks
- bridge issue data remains present after migration

Landing boundary:
- one visible git commit in a dedicated bridge worktree, plus the migrated local tracker state

title: SIGNING: extend the signer-session contract for composite open-session artifacts
description:
Goal:
Extend the bridge-owned signer/session contract so a session-class opener can return the real Tempo open-session artifact family instead of a single signature or opaque tx ref.

Scope:
- define the composite artifact needed for session open: signed transaction bytes, deterministic channelId, cumulativeAmount=0, and initial voucher signature
- keep the work at the contract/type surface first
- line it up with SECRET-0002 realization and the existing signer-session contract
- use the sharpened downstream proof from home-6e7 as the motivating case

Non-goals:
- no downstream home-specific runtime wiring
- no remote publication
- no generic wallet UX work
notes:
Lane launched in .git-worktrees/bridge-5ni-open-session-artifact from main for contract-only work over the composite open-session artifact boundary.
Visible commit 73255c6 adds the typed SessionOpenArtifact surface for composite open-session settlement material over SECRET-0002 realization, wires it into TYPE-FAMILIES and BRIDGE-FLOW, and refreshes the secret-0002 checksum manifest. Verification passed: scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh; scripts/bridge-property-check.sh. Local main is now at 73255c6.

title: Adopt Premath IDENTITY-ADMISSION kernel in bridge profile note
description:
Goal:
Refresh the bridge identity-admission profile so it explicitly adopts Premath raw/IDENTITY-ADMISSION as the reusable kernel owner rather than only referring to a generic upstream admission core.

Scope:
- add the bridge-owned identity-admission profile note to the live tree if it is still branch-only
- update the note so it names Premath raw/IDENTITY-ADMISSION as the reusable kernel owner
- keep bridge-domain ownership explicit for Authority, Admission, and Realization families
- add or refresh the stable README/index link

Non-goals:
- no schema churn unless a concrete gap is exposed
- no provider/runtime implementation work
- no attempt to move Premath doctrine into bridge

Verification:
- the note explicitly points at Premath raw/IDENTITY-ADMISSION as kernel owner
- bridge README/spec links resolve
- scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh passes

Landing boundary:
- one visible commit in bridge with the note plus link updates
notes: Landed in visible commit adf68b470163: added specs/bridge-adapter/identity-admission-profile.md, linked it from specs/bridge-adapter/README.md, and refreshed the adapter checksum manifest so the bridge profile explicitly adopts Premath raw/IDENTITY-ADMISSION as the reusable kernel owner.

title: Profile identity-admission semantics onto bridge Authority, Admission, and Realization families
description:
## Goal
Add a bridge-owned profile note that maps the upstream local identity/admission semantic core onto the existing bridge type families and handoff objects.

## Scope
- map principal presentation, assertion, admission, entitlements, and obstructions onto bridge-owned surfaces
- cover the path `AuthorizeRequest -> ProviderResults -> PolicyInput -> Decision -> MaterializationPlanRequest -> MaterializationSession`
- keep the work as a bridge profile note first
- add an index/reference link from the active bridge spec surface

## Non-goals
- do not move the general identity semantic core out of Fish
- do not redefine Premath kernel doctrine inside bridge
- do not start with schema churn unless the profile note exposes a concrete gap
- do not widen into downstream runtime or provider implementation work

## Verification
- the note cleanly maps identity-admission terms onto existing bridge type families
- the note preserves the boundary between upstream kernel law, bridge-domain profile meaning, and downstream runtime proof
- scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh passes after the note and index link land

## Landing boundary
Stop when bridge has one new profile note plus one stable reference link in the active spec surface and both are committed in one visible change.
close_reason: Completed in visible commit c8cc04c8c68e122952abe44d51255615dbca6357 on branch bridge-z3i-identity-admission-profile

title: Pull codex tooling from tusk in bridge flake
description: Wire bridge flake to expose repo-pinned codex/beads from shared llm-agents+tusk inputs, mirroring the established aac pattern.
design: Add llm-agents and tusk inputs with follows wiring; add codex/beads packages and apps; include packages in default devShell.
acceptance_criteria: nix run .#codex -- --help resolves; devShell includes codex+beads; README mentions codex entrypoint

title: PUBLISH: attach a bridge git remote for flake consumers
description:
Goal:
Attach a canonical Git publish remote for bridge so downstream flake consumers can lock a non-machine-local bridge input.

Scope:
- choose and configure the canonical publish remote for this repo
- document the preferred flake ref form for downstream consumers
- verify that a downstream repo can reference bridge by a published Git-backed flake input instead of a local path or git+file dogfood ref

Non-goals:
- do not change the bridge skill content again here
- do not change downstream consumer wiring in this issue
- do not widen into bridge runtime or deployment work

Acceptance:
- bridge has a configured publish remote
- the documented consumer ref points at that published remote with ?ref=main
- a downstream consumer can lock bridge without a machine-local source path
notes: Configured canonical publish remote origin=git@github.com:workingdoge/bridge.git, created public repo https://github.com/workingdoge/bridge, pushed main, and verified both flake and flake=false consumers can lock git+https://github.com/workingdoge/bridge.git?ref=main without a machine-local path.
close_reason: completed with public remote origin=git@github.com:workingdoge/bridge.git, pushed main, and visible doc commit e19fe52 in bridge-2kk-publish-remote

title: SKILL-DEV: export a portable bridge skill surface for flake consumers
description:
Goal:
Make the canonical bridge skill consumable by other repos without machine-local absolute paths. Bridge owns the exported surface; consumers lock a published bridge flake ref instead of pointing at a local checkout.

Scope:
- expose one bridge-owned flake surface for the canonical bridge skill bundle
- document the consumer contract for that exported surface
- name and provide a non-machine-local flake reference path that consumers can lock
- keep bridge as the canonical authoring home for the shared bridge skill

Non-goals:
- do not rewrite bridge-domain doctrine or widen the skill beyond its current canonical content
- do not move bridge-domain ownership into tusk or any consumer repo
- do not implement materialization runtime, providers, or funded deployment proof here

Acceptance:
- bridge exports one consumer-facing flake surface for the canonical bridge skill bundle
- the export contract is documented in-repo
- a non-machine-local flake ref is named or made available for downstream consumers
- a downstream consumer can stop relying on an absolute /Users/... path once this lands

Verification:
- nix flake metadata path:. --json
- rg -n 'bridge-skill|skill bundle|SKILL.md' flake.nix README.md .agents/skills/bridge
- one downstream consumer issue can point at the published contract without a /Users/... source path

Landing Boundary:
- stop at one visible bridge commit plus a named export contract for consumers

title: BRIDGE: define the observation-interpretation family between Authority and Admission
description:
Goal:
Define the canonical bridge-domain interpretation family that assigns stable meaning to foreign observations after authoritative fact resolution and before admission assembly.

Scope:
- add one bridge-domain note that names the observation-interpretation boundary as a first-class family between Authority and Admission
- define the high-level flow from foreign observation to source identity or versioning to interpretation binding to typed observation to admission
- make explicit that decoder registries, caches, and parser selection are subordinate implementation mechanisms, not the semantic center
- explain stronger-than-address source identity and validity-window requirements for interpretation binding
- keep the note general across contract logs, provider payloads, versioned webhooks, and other foreign observation families
- update the bridge type-family map to include Interpretation

Non-goals:
- do not implement runtime storage or cache behavior in this issue
- do not widen into Kurma carrier/runtime realization here
- do not add executable scripting or dynamic decoder semantics
- do not revise secret realization or planner surfaces in this issue

Simplex:
- kind: 1-simplex
- common contract: foreign observation interpretation binding
- vertex A: bridge as canonical meaning owner
- vertex B: kurma as runtime/carrier consumer

Verification:
- bd show <issue-id> --json
- test -f specs/bridge-adapter/interpretation-binding.md
- scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh

Landing Boundary:
- stop at one tracked Bridge issue, one interpretation-binding note, and one type-family-map update that make the new family explicit
acceptance_criteria:
- Bridge has a tracked issue for the observation-interpretation family
- the spec surface names Interpretation as a family between Authority and Admission
- the landed note includes a high-level topology for foreign observation -> interpretation binding -> typed observation -> admission
- the note treats decoder registry and cache as subordinate runtime mechanisms rather than the semantic center
- the note states stronger-than-address identity and validity-window requirements for admissible interpretation binding
notes: Landed specs/bridge-adapter/interpretation-binding.md as the canonical bridge-domain note for foreign observation interpretation between Authority and Admission. Updated specs/TYPE-FAMILIES.md to insert Interpretation as a first-class family and updated specs/bridge-adapter/README.md so the adapter surface points at the new family. Refreshed specs/bridge-adapter/SHA256SUMS.txt for the added note and README change, then verified with nix run .#bridge-conformance-check.
